title: Extreme heat from climate change linked to smaller babies
content: Exposure to extreme heat conditions is resulting in more babies being born with low birth weight, according to a new study involving Adelaide University researchers.
snippet: Exposure to extreme heat conditions is resulting in more babies being born with low birth weight, according to a new study involving Adelaide University researchers.

title: The platform that wants to save Pakistan’s groundwater
content: The Groundwater Management Information System is bringing data, institutions and farmers together to save a resource at risk.
snippet: The Groundwater Management Information System is bringing data, institutions and farmers together to save a resource at risk.

title: 2 trillion gallons of water trigger historic flooding in Hawaiʻi
content: More than 2 trillion gallons of water—enough to fill 3 million Olympic-sized swimming pools—inundated Hawaiʻi in March.
snippet: More than 2 trillion gallons of water—enough to fill 3 million Olympic-sized swimming pools—inundated Hawaiʻi in March.

title: UNEP FI makes climate scenario data accessible and actionable for financial institutions
content: UNEP FI is pleased to announce the release of its Climate Pathways Navigator, a unique tool that gives financial institutions direct access to the climate scenario data they need to make informed, science-based decisions on their decarbonization pathways.

title: Systems Change x Behavioural Science for Climate Action
content: The Systems Change x Behavioural Science for Climate Action course empowers young leaders to address climate challenges through systems thinking and behavioural science.
snippet: The Systems Change x Behavioural Science for Climate Action course empowers young leaders to address climate challenges through systems thinking and behavioural science.

title: Test... Test... Test
content: During the month of March, states throughout the U.S. recognized severe weather awareness week by conducting tornado drills.
snippet: During the month of March, states throughout the U.S. recognized severe weather awareness week by conducting tornado drills.

title: Who pays the price for Japan’s nuclear power?
content: Because Fukushima is considered a national responsibility, it is legally stipulated that all contaminated soil must be removed from the prefecture by 2045.
snippet: Because Fukushima is considered a national responsibility, it is legally stipulated that all contaminated soil must be removed from the prefecture by 2045.

title: CIMMYT and Uzbekistan strengthen partnership to advance climate-resilient wheat systems
content: The Government of Uzbekistan and CIMMYT sign a Letter of Intent to support wheat sector modernization and food security.
snippet: The Government of Uzbekistan and CIMMYT sign a Letter of Intent to support wheat sector modernization and food security.

title: The Valorada Project: Improving access and usability of climate data
content: Regions and municipalities routinely generate extensive volumes of non-climate data – such as socio-economic, demographic, land use and satellite data, environmental and infrastructure datasets.
snippet: Regions and municipalities routinely generate extensive volumes of non-climate data – such as socio-economic, demographic, land use and satellite data, environmental and infrastructure datasets.

title: Index reveals global water resources' growing dependence on extreme rainfall
content: As global temperatures climb, rainfall patterns are shifting in ways that could put water resources and agriculture under increasing strain, a study published in Water Resources Research suggests.
snippet: As global temperatures climb, rainfall patterns are shifting in ways that could put water resources and agriculture under increasing strain, a study published in Water Resources Research suggests.

title: How mine action clears a path to climate resilience
content: In Africa’s Sahel region, where communities are affected by both insecurity and environmental degradation, climate-induced displacement is increasing exposure to explosive hazards. At the same time, flooding and shifting landscapes can move or expose UXO.

title: Tunisia: Major push to strengthen water security and resilience

title: Fifth of arabica coffee growing area to be unsuitable by 2050, report says
content: Climate change could result in 20% of current arabica coffee growing areas being classified as unsuitable by 2050, Rabobank forecast in ​a report on Monday.
snippet: Climate change could result in 20% of current arabica coffee growing areas being classified as unsuitable by 2050, Rabobank forecast in a report on Monday.

title: Pocket gardens: The tiny urban oases with surprisingly big benefits
content: A simple patch of greenery in a city may seem like a blip in the concrete jungle, but it’s an extremely powerful way to solve a bunch of problems at once.
snippet: A simple patch of greenery in a city may seem like a blip in the concrete jungle, but it’s an extremely powerful way to solve a bunch of problems at once.

title: Bigger storms, more often: study projects likely future rainfall impacts on NZ
content: Study points to a future where extreme rainfall is both more intense and more frequent across much of New Zealand – with some simulated storms bearing the hallmarks of weather disasters from Aotearoa’s past.

title: Winter’s alarmingly low snowpack offers a glimpse of the changing rhythm of water in the western US
content: The April 1 snowpack measurement has long been the single most important number in western water management, considered a strong proxy for how much water the mountains are holding in reserve. But in 2026, that savings account has been woefully deficient.

title: We have the proof that logging makes Tasmania’s forests more flammable
content: W.D. Jackson has suggested that after Tasmania’s wet eucalypt forests qre burned by severe bushfires, there would be a high-risk period during their regrowth when they are at risk of severely burning again.

title: UNEP Copenhagen Climate Center

title: Continental United States direct Atlantic tropical cyclone fatalities: 1963–2024
content: Tropical cyclone (TC) fatality attribution is critical for understanding changing risk patterns, warning effectiveness, and informing emergency management strategies.
snippet: Tropical cyclone (TC) fatality attribution is critical for understanding changing risk patterns, warning effectiveness, and informing emergency management strategies.

title: Towards improved flood prediction: a review of deterministic hydrologic-hydraulic model coupling
content: This scoping review synthesised 94 peer-reviewed studies published between 1994 and 2024, applying the PRISMA framework to trace the evolution of deterministic hydrologic–hydraulic model coupling for flood forecasting.

title: ASEAN capacity building roadmap 2025 – 2030 on disaster management
content: The ASEAN Capacity Building Roadmap on Disaster Management is a strategic framework designed to enhance the capacities of ASEAN Member States in meeting the challenges of a changing humanitarian landscape due to new and emerging threats.

title: Towards a safer and resilient Nagaland: what we do, why we do, and how we learn
content: This publication explores how Nagaland is building a more resilient and disaster-ready system through coordinated efforts involving government institutions, local communities, and international partners.

title: A nation of disasters: lessons on systemic risks and cascading impacts from Malawi's COVID-19 experience
content: This study shows how COVID-19, floods, and cholera interacted in Malawi to produce cascading social, economic, and governance impacts, while also revealing the value of adaptive social protection and multi-hazard risk management.

title: A decision support framework for catchment-scale nature-based flood solutions using multi-objective particle swarm optimization
content: This study shows that afforestation as a nature-based solution can significantly reduce flood risk and economic damage while providing additional carbon and economic benefits when evaluated through an integrated decision-support framework.

title: A day in the life: working in the National Situation Room
content: The National Situation Room (NSR) is a whole-of-government all-hazards monitoring and reporting facility. Operating around the clock, it plays a crucial role in safeguarding the nation.
snippet: The National Situation Room (NSR) is a whole-of-government all-hazards monitoring and reporting facility. Operating around the clock, it plays a crucial role in safeguarding the nation.

title: What a 1,000-year-old drainage system teaches us about flood risk
content: Ganzhou’s ancient Fushougou system offers a model of integrated, nature-based flood management for modern communities.
snippet: Ganzhou’s ancient Fushougou system offers a model of integrated, nature-based flood management for modern communities.

title: Tropical volcanoes and Asian drought
content: Researchers, including those from the University of Tokyo, combine various past climate data to investigate the impacts of ancient volcanic eruptions.
snippet: Researchers, including those from the University of Tokyo, combine various past climate data to investigate the impacts of ancient volcanic eruptions.

title: Climate science has no place in scientific reference manual for judges, attorneys general say
content: A chapter on climate science has been removed from a manual designed to be an independent, neutral source of scientific information for judges.
snippet: A chapter on climate science has been removed from a manual designed to be an independent, neutral source of scientific information for judges.

title: Does fighting climate change put the economy at risk?
content: A study led by the UOC indicates that fiscal crisis risks and climate risks are closely related, and highlights institutional quality as a key factor in their mitigation.
snippet: A study led by the UOC indicates that fiscal crisis risks and climate risks are closely related, and highlights institutional quality as a key factor in their mitigation.

title: Wildfires, storms, floods contribute to record 92% of global insured losses in 2025, says Swiss Re Institute
content: In 2025, secondary perils – among them wildfires, severe convective storms and floods – accounted for a record 92% of total global natural catastrophe insured losses of USD 107 billion.
snippet: In 2025, secondary perils – among them wildfires, severe convective storms and floods – accounted for a record 92% of total global natural catastrophe insured losses of USD 107 billion.

title: How declining cloudiness is accelerating global warming
content: As global temperatures have reached record highs in recent years, there has been concern that the decline in cloudiness may be enhancing warming more than previously expected.
snippet: As global temperatures have reached record highs in recent years, there has been concern that the decline in cloudiness may be enhancing warming more than previously expected.

title: Water flow in prairie watersheds is increasingly unpredictable — but AI could help
content: In recent years, the Prairies have seen bigger swings in climate conditions — very wet years followed by very dry ones. That makes an already unpredictable landscape even harder to forecast, with real consequences for flood preparedness and water quality.

title: New AI tool forecasts drought 90 days ahead nationwide
content: The USGS released a machine learning tool that forecasts droughts up to 90 days ahead nationwide. The tool may provide communities extra time to prepare for water shortages that could impact agriculture, municipal supplies, recreation and ecosystems.

title: Scaling adaptation finance: what works in LDCs and SIDS
content: Scaling adaptation finance is an urgent priority for climate-vulnerable countries – particularly LDCs and SIDS. This webinar brings together new evidence and real-world experience to explore what is working – and what needs to change.

title: The role of local government in disaster risk reduction
content: Local governments are central to reducing disaster risk and shaping safer, more resilient communities. The decisions councils make every day about land use, infrastructure, governance and community engagement can either lock in future risk.

title: The Papua New Guinea national disaster risk reduction framework (2017–2030)
content: The Papua New Guinea National Disaster Risk Reduction Framework (2017–2030) outlines the country’s long-term strategy to reduce disaster risk and strengthen resilience.
snippet: The Papua New Guinea National Disaster Risk Reduction Framework (2017–2030) outlines the country’s long-term strategy to reduce disaster risk and strengthen resilience.
